LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FAILS TO ALLEGE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DISABILITY DISCRIMINATION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS SHOWING THAT SHE IS DISABLED WITHIN THE MEANING OF THE ADA. THE 42 USC 1983 CIVIL RIGHTS CLAIMS FAIL TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT HER RIGHTS WERE VIOLATED AS THE RESULT OF A CCSF POLICY OR CUSTOM. SEE MONELL V. DEPT. OF SOCIAL SERVICES OF CITY OF NEW YORK, 436 U.S. 658, 691 (1978). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: PAULA SHI #13358.

Off Calendar Feb-17-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff CAROL A. TRUFANT, Tel No. (510) 224-4504 appearing in pro per;
Deputy City Attorney ELIZABETH PEDERSON of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel No. (415) 554-3862 appearing for Defendant City and County of San Francisco.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CAROL TRUFANT, PHD'S Motion To Arrest Judgment Of 5-23-14 Pursuant To Order Continuing Case Management Conference Of 8-25-14 is denied. Judgment was entered in favor of Defendant on November 25, 2014. This motion does not establish a basis to set aside that judgment. To the extent that Plaintiff is seeking reconsideration of the Court's order granting judgment on the pleadings, such a motion is time-barred. (See CCP sec. 1008.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
Off Calendar May-07-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Carol Trufant, 510.221.4504, plaintiff in propria persona.
Elizabeth Pederson, Deputy City Attorney, 415.554.3800, for defendant City and County of San Francisco.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings is granted without leave to amend. The Second Amended Complaint fails to allege a cause of action for disability discrimination because plaintiff does not allege sufficient facts showing that she is disabled within the meaning of the ADA. The 42 USC 1983 civil rights claims fail to state a cause of action because plaintiff fails to allege that her rights were violated as the result of a CCSF policy or custom. See Monell v. Dept. of Social Services of City of New York, 436 U.S. 658, 691 (1978). Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Paula Shi #13358.
